sowing.method: Planting of rooted cuttings or rootstocks on trellises

seed_rate.notes: Optimal vine density of 2000-2500 vines per hectare under Bower training system.

sowing.soil_conditions: Well-drained sandy loam to gravelly soils with pH 6.5-7.5.

irrigation.notes: Provide daily drip irrigation to support early foliage shoot expansion.
Regulated irrigation is required. Dry stress causes severe flower drop.
Support expansion size of berries. Reduce irrigation before harvest to increase TSS/sugar content.

fertilizer.notes: Apply high phosphorus and potassium along with farmyard manure.
Potassium application is split to ensure high TSS and flavor.

maturity_indicators: Berries turn translucent and seed turns brown
Brix/sugar level reaches 18-20 degrees on a refractometer
harvest.harvest_method: Hand clipping of bunches using sharp shears
harvest.notes: Sort bunches, remove decayed berries, and pre-cool harvested grapes immediately to 2-4°C.
